channel.title: Air Express HVAC — Field Notes
channel.description: Utah-specific heating and cooling advice from the Air Express HVAC team in Lehi.

item.title: The Fall Furnace Checklist We Actually Do Before Utah Winter Hits
item.description: Every October we get the same panicked call: 'The heat just stopped.' Here's what our technicians check in September that keeps those calls from happening.
item.author: noreply@airexpresshvac.net (The Air Express Team)

item.title: The Plain-English HVAC Glossary for Utah Homeowners
item.description: Every HVAC term you'll see on a quote, an inspection report, or a sales pitch — defined in plain English by the people who actually install the equipment.
item.author: noreply@airexpresshvac.net (The Air Express Team)

item.title: Heat Pump vs. Furnace in Utah: The Honest Comparison
item.description: Utility ads say heat pumps. Old-school HVAC guys say furnaces. Here's what actually makes sense for a Utah house — with the math, the tradeoffs, and the honest answer.
item.author: noreply@airexpresshvac.net (The Air Express Team)

item.title: Why Your Utah AC Runs All Day in July (and What Actually Helps)
item.description: Your July power bill isn't your AC's fault — it's usually three things in your attic, your ducts, and your windows. Here's how we actually diagnose it.
item.author: noreply@airexpresshvac.net (The Air Express Team)

item.title: What Indoor Air Quality Actually Means at 4,500 Feet
item.description: Utah's inversion winters and dry summers make indoor air a different problem than anywhere else. Here's what actually works — and what doesn't — at Wasatch Front altitude.
item.author: noreply@airexpresshvac.net (The Air Express Team)

item.title: Geothermal in Utah: Brutally Honest About When It Makes Sense
item.description: Geothermal heat pumps are a real thing, they work in Utah, and they're almost always the wrong answer for the house you're in right now. Here's why.
item.author: noreply@airexpresshvac.net (The Air Express Team)

item.title: What HVAC Actually Costs in Utah (Real Numbers, No Games)
item.description: Every HVAC cost article online hides behind 'prices vary.' Here are the real numbers we quote in Lehi, Eagle Mountain, and Saratoga Springs in 2026. Ranges included.
item.author: noreply@airexpresshvac.net (The Air Express Team)

item.title: Spring AC Prep: What We Actually Check Before Utah Hits 100°F
item.description: The Utah Valley goes from cold mornings to 100-degree afternoons in about six weeks. Here's what we walk through every April so your AC is ready when the switch flips.
item.author: noreply@airexpresshvac.net (The Air Express Team)

item.title: The Utah Homeowner's HVAC Buying Guide (From Someone Who Writes the Quotes)
item.description: Most HVAC buying guides are written by marketing departments. This one's written by the people who actually install the systems — and see which ones fail after five years.
item.author: noreply@airexpresshvac.net (The Air Express Team)

item.title: Furnace Not Heating? Ten Things to Check Before You Call Us
item.description: Half the emergency furnace calls we get in January are problems the homeowner could have fixed in three minutes. Here's the checklist we walk through before we drive out.
item.author: noreply@airexpresshvac.net (The Air Express Team)
